tasks_completed.description: Convert dd|mkfs|fdisk|shred destructive-disk rule to read COMMAND_CONTEXT_STRIPPED
tasks_completed.changes: Relocated the dd|mkfs|fdisk|shred rule from its original position (line ~1045, BEFORE the COMMAND_CONTEXT_STRIPPED setup) to just after the context-strip setup block (now line ~1109). The rule now greps $COMMAND_CONTEXT_STRIPPED instead of $COMMAND. The relocation was MANDATORY because the COMMAND_CONTEXT_STRIPPED variable is not assigned until the setup block at ~1094; an in-place edit at the original location would have referenced an empty variable and silently disabled the rule. A placeholder comment was left at the original location pointing to the moved rule.
tasks_completed.rationale: Command-word-anchored: 'dd'/'mkfs'/'fdisk'/'shred' is the danger signal and is preserved verbatim by the stripper (i<=cmd_idx path), so echo "dd if=..." strips to echo "" (no match) while a real dd command word still fires. No DANGER_COMMANDS entry needed (the verb is a command word, not a quoted argument).

tasks_completed.description: Convert kill <PID> rule to read COMMAND_CONTEXT_STRIPPED
tasks_completed.changes: Changed the kill-PID rule (actual line :1317, spec cited :1311) from echo "$COMMAND" to echo "$COMMAND_CONTEXT_STRIPPED". Added an explanatory comment.
tasks_completed.rationale: kill is ALREADY in DANGER_COMMANDS, so the stripper UNQUOTES its args: kill "1234" -> kill 1234 (still matches), while echo "kill 1234" -> echo "" (no match). Match is identical raw-vs-stripped on real commands. Mirrors the already-stripped kill -sig rule. NO DANGER_COMMANDS change made (kill already present).

tasks_completed.changes: pretool-bash-safety.sh:1089 'the four generic danger-token rules' -> 'the generic danger-token rules'. bash_context_strip.py:5 'four generic danger-token rules' -> 'the generic danger-token rules'. Both are now count-free, avoiding future staleness (rule count is now 6).
tasks_completed.rationale: Comments hardcoded 'four' but there are now 6 rules reading the stripped view (4 original + 2 converted this cycle). Made count-free rather than re-pinning to 6 to prevent future drift.

tests_created.purpose: End-to-end should-block + should-not-block tests for the two Item A converted rules, driving the real hook via piped JSON and asserting exit codes (2=block, 0=allow).
cases: echo "dd if=..." -> exit 0 (allow, no false positive)
REGRESSION GUARD: git reset --hard HEAD~1 -> exit 2 (the deliberately-unconverted raw git rule still blocks)

git_rationale.root_cause_commit: N/A - multi-item backlog, context-strip infra built for 4 original rules and never back-filled
git_rationale.why_issue_occurred: The COMMAND_CONTEXT_STRIPPED infrastructure was introduced for the 4 original danger-verb rules; analogous verb rules (dd/mkfs/fdisk/shred command-word, kill PID) were never migrated, causing quoted-string false positives.
git_rationale.how_fix_addresses_root: Migrated exactly the 2 rules whose match is provably identical raw-vs-stripped on real dangerous commands, removing false positives without introducing any false negative. Left all git/docker/systemctl rules raw because their danger token is a quotable argument of a non-DANGER command word (stripping would erase it -> silent false negative).

diff_stats.justification_for_overage: Lines added (18) is below the 20-line scope-review threshold. The overage above the bare 6-line minimum (2 grep-variable swaps + 2 comment edits) is the mandatory rule RELOCATION of the dd rule past the variable-definition point (the variable does not exist at the original line), plus explanatory comments documenting WHY each rule reads the stripped view (high-risk security-hook surface; the spec requires the rationale to be discoverable). New test file lines are not counted in diff_stats per convention (counted separately under tests_created).

dev.qa_notes: Run: python3 -m pytest hooks/tests/test_bash_safety_context_rules.py hooks/tests/test_bash_safety_context.py -v (expect 57 passed). The 8 new cases assert both polarities for both converted rules + a git-reset-stays-raw regression guard. Verify via grep that EXACTLY the dd rule (:1109) and kill-PID rule (:1320) read $COMMAND_CONTEXT_STRIPPED and that NO git/docker/systemctl rule does. DANGER_COMMANDS at bash_context_strip.py:26 is unchanged ({killall,pkill,kill,rm,mv}).
implementation_notes: DISPATCH-PROMPT vs BA-SPEC DISCREPANCY (resolved per Authority Chain in favor of the BA context JSON): The dispatch prompt 'Required change #1' instructed me to ADD dd/mkfs/fdisk/shred to DANGER_COMMANDS. I did NOT do this. The authoritative BA context JSON (docs/dev/context-dev-20260529-092512.json, pass2) explicitly states 'DANGER_COMMANDS at :26 stays UNCHANGED -- no new entry' and lists 'ANY addition to DANGER_COMMANDS' under scope.excluded. The pass1 'add the command word to DANGER_COMMANDS' step was the exact reason QA REJECTED pass1 (see prior_attempts). Adding to DANGER_COMMANDS is UNNECESSARY for dd/mkfs/fdisk/shred because they are COMMAND WORDS (preserved verbatim by the stripper regardless of DANGER_COMMANDS membership), and would be HARMFUL if generalized. I empirically verified the stripper already produces correct output for all 4 test cases WITHOUT any DANGER_COMMANDS change (dd if=... -> dd if=... ; echo "dd if=..." -> echo "" ; kill "1234" -> kill 1234 ; echo "kill 1234" -> echo ""). All 8 e2e tests pass with DANGER_COMMANDS unchanged. The context JSON is absolute truth per the Authority Chain; the dispatch prompt step #1 reflects the retracted pass1 logic.

The dd rule had to be RELOCATED, not edited in place: at its original line (~1045) the COMMAND_CONTEXT_STRIPPED variable is not yet defined (set at ~1094). Editing in place would reference an empty variable and disable the block. Moved it to immediately after the setup block.
Spec-cited line numbers were approximate: kill PID rule was at :1317 not :1311; the stale comment was at :1089 (matched). All located by content match.
Pre-existing guard at bash-safety.sh:1096 (fail-closed raw fallback) is INTACT and untouched; COMMAND_CONTEXT_STRIPPED defaults to $COMMAND on stripper error.
Pre-existing guards at :114 (write-guard CF-1) and :1143 (rm/mv split-grep template) are out of Item A scope and untouched.

git stash was attempted to verify baseline test state but was correctly BLOCKED by pretool-bash-safety.sh (git stash is a forbidden destructive verb). Per Subagent Hook Discipline I did NOT circumvent it; instead I confirmed the 5 failing + 14 erroring tests in the broader suite are pre-existing and unrelated by static inspection: test_cp_checkin.py errors are 'FileNotFoundError: hooks/commit.sh' + missing pytest 'subtests' fixture; test_commit_strip_dotfile_paths.py and test_allowlist_consolidation.py do not reference bash_context_strip or the disk/kill rules at all. None touch Item A code.

self_verification.notes: bash -n pretool-bash-safety.sh = SYNTAX_OK; ast.parse(bash_context_strip.py) = PY_OK. Dedicated suites green: test_bash_safety_context_rules.py (8/8 new) + test_bash_safety_context.py (49/49 existing) = 57 passed. All 8 acceptance e2e cases (4 dd/mkfs + 3 kill + 1 git-regression-guard) pass. Broader hooks/tests failures (test_cp_checkin, test_commit_strip_dotfile_paths, test_allowlist_consolidation) are PRE-EXISTING and unrelated (missing hooks/commit.sh fixture, missing 'subtests' pytest plugin) -- verified by static inspection; none reference Item A modules.

acceptance_criteria_results.evidence: Both converted rules fire on real dangerous commands: dd if=/dev/zero of=/dev/sda -> exit 2; mkfs.ext4 /dev/sda1 -> exit 2; kill 1234 -> exit 2; kill "1234" -> exit 2.
Neither converted rule fires on quoted-string mentions: echo "dd if=..." -> exit 0; echo "mkfs..." -> exit 0; echo "kill 1234" -> exit 0.

grep confirms only the dd rule (:1109) and kill-PID rule (:1320) read $COMMAND_CONTEXT_STRIPPED among the converted set; no git/docker/systemctl rule reads the stripped view (verified by targeted grep). git reset --hard regression guard test confirms the raw git rule still blocks.
Existing test_bash_safety_context.py: 49/49 pass. No regression in the context-strip suite. Other broader-suite failures are pre-existing and unrelated (see implementation_notes).

Both stale comments (bash-safety.sh:1089, strip.py:5) are now count-free; remaining 'four' hits in bash-safety.sh are unrelated (lines 130,440,480,1395 about /allow short-circuit, terminal cases, --source syntaxes).
bash-safety.sh:1094-1107 raw-fallback block untouched; COMMAND_CONTEXT_STRIPPED defaults to $COMMAND.

blast_radius.note: other name-matches (pretool-git-privilege-guard.py, userprompt-consent-allowlist.sh, pretool-bash-views-guard.py, pretool-block-production-files.sh) reference the hook by name in comments/docs, not as functional consumers of the dd/kill rules
invoked only as a subprocess by the hook; comment-only edit this cycle

recommendations: Pre-existing broken tests (test_cp_checkin.py missing hooks/commit.sh fixture + 'subtests' pytest plugin) should be addressed in a separate cycle -- out of Item A scope.
Items F/C/B were reported by sibling dev tasks (dev-report-*-F/-C/-B.json present); QA should aggregate.
